scenes.text: Mia and Sam loved Saturdays. On Saturdays they went to the market with their puppy, Pepper. Pepper was small and brown, with floppy ears and a bright red collar.
scenes.dominant_action: Mia and Sam walk toward a sunny market with their small brown puppy Pepper on a red leash, his tail wagging.

scenes.text: The market was busy and loud. People talked and laughed. There were flowers, fish, and warm bread. Pepper sniffed everything. His little tail would not stop wagging.
scenes.dominant_action: The bustling market square with flower, fish, and bread stalls; Pepper sniffs everything, his little tail wagging.

scenes.text: Mia held the leash tight. "Stay close, Pepper," she said. Sam giggled. "He wants to see it all!"
scenes.dominant_action: Mia holds Pepper's red leash tightly while Sam laughs beside her; Pepper looks eager to explore everything.

scenes.text: Then a yellow butterfly flew past Pepper's nose. Pepper jumped. He pulled hard. The leash slipped right out of Mia's hand.
scenes.dominant_action: A yellow butterfly flits past Pepper's nose; he lunges after it and the red leash slips from Mia's hand.

scenes.text: "Pepper, wait!" Mia called. But Pepper ran into the crowd. In one blink, he was gone.
scenes.dominant_action: Pepper dashes away into the crowd and disappears as Mia reaches out after him.

scenes.text: Sam's eyes went wide. "Where did he go?" Mia took his hand. "Don't worry," she said. "We will find him. Let's look together."
scenes.dominant_action: Sam looks frightened with wide eyes; Mia kneels and takes his hand, calmly reassuring him they will search together.

scenes.text: First they ran to the flower cart. The flowers smelled sweet. A lady was selling red roses. But there was no little puppy here.
scenes.dominant_action: Mia and Sam search at a colorful flower cart where a lady sells red roses; there is no puppy in sight.

scenes.text: Next they checked the fish stand. A big gray cat sat on a box. The cat hissed. Sam jumped back. Still, no Pepper.
scenes.dominant_action: At the fish stand a big gray cat hisses from a box; Sam jumps back in surprise, and still there is no Pepper.

scenes.text: They went to the bakery last. The bread smelled warm and good. Sam's chin began to wobble. "What if we never find him?" he whispered.
scenes.dominant_action: Outside a warm bakery stall, Sam's chin wobbles as he worries aloud that they might never find Pepper.

scenes.text: Just then, they heard it. A happy bark! "Pepper!" they both shouted. They followed the sound around the corner.
scenes.dominant_action: Mia and Sam perk up at a happy bark and rush together around a market corner toward the sound.

scenes.text: A kind fruit seller was holding Pepper safe in her arms. She smiled. "Is this your puppy? He came to say hello. I saw his red collar and waited for you."
scenes.dominant_action: A kind fruit seller smiles warmly, holding the safe brown puppy Pepper, his red collar showing, gently in her arms.

scenes.text: Mia and Sam hugged Pepper tight. Pepper licked their faces and wagged his tail. "Thank you!" they said to the kind lady.
scenes.dominant_action: Mia and Sam hug Pepper tightly as he licks their faces and wags his tail; they thank the kind fruit seller.

scenes.text: They walked home together, hand in hand, with Pepper close by. That night, Pepper curled up between them and fell fast asleep. It had been the best Saturday of all.
scenes.dominant_action: That evening the children walk home with Pepper; that night the puppy sleeps curled up safe between them.
